growth_records.medium: Gas substrate: Syngas: 20% CO, 5% H2, 15% CO2, 60% N2
growth_records.cite: Vees et al. Towards continuous industrial bioprocessing with solventogenic and acetogenic clostridia: challenges, progress and perspectives. Journal of Industrial Microbiology & Biotechnology (2020). DOI:10.1007/s10295-020-02296-2. [PMC7658081]
growth_records.snippet: C. carboxidivorans P7 | Hollow fiber membrane biofilm reactor | membrane | Syngas 20%CO/5%H2/15%CO2/60%N2 | Ethanol 23.93 g/L, 0.24 mol C/mol C
growth_records.notes: SECONDARY DATA compiled in review Table; original citation: Shen Y, Brown R, Wen Z. Syngas fermentation of Clostridium carboxidivoran P7 in a hollow fiber membrane biofilm reactor: evaluating the mass transfer coefficien. Solventogenic/acetogenic Clostridium, anaerobic. Dilution rate D reported; growth rate NOT equal to D (multi-stage / cell-retention / immobilized -> growth uncoupled from D). Volumetric productivity (g/L/h) and yield (g/g) are the reported measured rates. Immobilized-cell / biofilm continuous reactor: only maximum volumetric productivity reported; no dilution rate, CDW, or growth rate. Growth uncoupled from reactor throughput. Carrier: adsorption, membrane. Continuous mode approximated as 'chemostat' (no biofilm enum).

up.method: gas monitoring; 73% of initial CO in bottles consumed after 4 days of incubation; CO consumption coupled to growth and stopped at stationary phase; rate not resolved per gDW/h (batch, no biomass-normalized time-series in text)
reassimilation observed after several days of growth (secondary uptake)
reassimilation observed to a lower degree after several days of growth

sec.method: HPLC/analytical; major product; appeared after 24 h
produced during growth (coupled to CO consumption); not quantified per unit
only traces detected at stationary phase

growth_records.cite: Bruant G et al. Genomic Analysis of Carbon Monoxide Utilization and Butanol Production by Clostridium carboxidivorans Strain P7T. PLoS ONE (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0013033. [PMC2946384]
growth_records.snippet: After a 24 h lag phase, P7T grew and reached a plateau (OD600 of 0.77) after four days at 37C with 100% CO. After four days, 73% of the CO was consumed. Acetate was the major product with 56.58 mmol produced per mol of CO consumed, followed
growth_records.notes: Single experimental condition: batch autotrophic gas fermentation on 100% CO, strictly anaerobic, 37C, initial pH 6.0, 20 mL medium in 120 mL serum bottles, 100 rpm. Product formation reported as YIELDS (mmol product per mol CO consumed): acetate 56.58, ethanol 38.21, butyrate 36.45, butanol 8.91; acetone not detected. CO uptake given only as 73% of initial CO consumed after 4 days (not biomass/time-normalized), so no qMFA/qS in mmol/gDW/h derivable. Growth: 24 h lag then plateau OD600=0.77 after 4 days; no exponential-phase specific growth rate or biomass concentration reported, so growth_rate/doubling_time cannot be computed. Later reassimilation (uptake) of secreted acetate and butyrate observed. No 13C-MFA performed. Values all explicit in Results (Figure 1B narrative).
